narrative: Iran’s closure of the Strait of Hormuz dominates the weekend tape, setting up a significant crude-driven risk-off bid for the Tokyo open. EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1419; the geopolitical escalation and upcoming US CPI is likely to weigh on the pair as safe-haven USD demand likely intensifies. USD/JPY closed Friday at 161.67; while JPY may see haven flows, the +255bp OIS carry advantage and hawkish Fed commentary from Kevin Warsh would support the pair at Sunday's open. NZD/CHF remains a critical bilateral theme following the RBNZ's first hike in three years (NZD 1W avg: +1.331%, CHF 1W avg: -0.710%, G10 spread: 2.041%).
